title: Human Gates, Permissions, and Reversible Autonomy · Independent Harness Trial
summary: Set human review points for a consequential operations agent. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a risk-tiered approval matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify actions by reversibility, blast radius, sensitivity, and external impact.
Place approvals immediately before consequential effects with enough evidence to decide.
Give each loop and tool only the capabilities and data required for its current goal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Emergency Stop applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Implement pause, cancel, revoke, and containment paths that work during tool execution.
